title: About this Site
text:
Hello! My name is Zach, you may know me as a teaching assistant in one of your courses. I made this website to help answer common questions I and my fellow TAs get.
I hope you find this website helpful, if you would like to contact me try emailing me using my university username or sending me a teams message. My network username isTWZ6852.
Please don’t use my zach.barrett@aut.ac.nz address. I do not check it.
I made this website using quarto and a custom theme based on stephango’s excellent flexoki colour-scheme.

title: Troubleshooting for Electronics Project
section: How to fix the getting kicked off workspace problem
text:
How to fix the getting kicked off workspace problem

Close Altium.
Open Altium
IMMEDIATELY OPEN COMPONENTS BEFORE ANYTHING ELSE OPENS
Reconnect to workspace
Login

title: Troubleshooting for Electronics Project
section: How to fix the lastconnection.data error (sometimes)
text:
How to fix the lastconnection.data error (sometimes)
We do not know why this works, only that it does most of the time. Please follow these steps as closely as possible.

Open the components menu.
Click the three horizontal lines (hamburger) button.
Select “libraries preferences”
Open the “Installed” tab.
Select all libraries.
Press “remove”.
Press “Install”.
Open the K drive libraries path.
Select all libraries under “Altium Libraries”.
Press “Open”.
Close Altium.
Reopen Altium
Immediately select the components tab
Log in.
Connect to the AUT workspace.

title: Troubleshooting for Electronics Project
section: How to add an External File in Altium
text:
How to add an External File in Altium
For a file to be properly included in an Altium project, it must meet three criteria:

It must be in the correct folder.

It must not be a referenced file.

It must be added to version control.


You must make sure that any externally added file meets these conditions.

Moving a file to the correct folder
Once you have your project open, right click on it and select “Explore”

This will open the file explorer location for the project you are working on.
Now, move the file from its original location into the project:

(Demo has been moved from downloads into here)


Adding the file to the project
Once that is done, right click on the project and click “Add existing to project”

Select the new file, and press “Open”.

Ensure the file icon does not have an arrow on it like this icon:  If it does, you need to go through the “Add existing to project” step again. Then, remove the old referenced file. Ensure you press “Exclude from project” instead of deleting it, to make sure that you can recover from mistakes on this step.


Once that is done, add it to version control by right-clicking it, going under
History and Version Control, and selecting “Add to Version Control”




Saving to Server
Finally, right click “Save to Server” next to your project, add a meaningful commit message, and save.


Checking you have done it correctly
Open up Altium 365 and check your project has the file you added, if not call over a TA.

title: Troubleshooting for Electronics Project
text:
Altium Libraries Path
This is the path you will use to install the K Drive libraries.
K:\WELLESLEY COPY\DESIGN & CREATIVE TECHNOLOGIES\School of Engineering\Electrical & Electronic Engineering\Resources\AltiumLibraries\
For projects one and two, you will use 2025S2.IntLib.

Routing Rules

title: Project One
text:
Project One Design
You will first need to create a new project, create a schematic file, and import the PCB template
Once you are done with that, recreate the schematic in altium. Check the Bill of Materials to make sure you are using the right components from the right libraries.

title: Project One
text:
Your task for Project One is to design and build a small line following robot.
You will be given a schematic to follow, (although you will need to recreate it yourself), and a template file for the PCB. You will then route the PCB according to the guidelines we give you.

title: Common Routing Errors
text:
ImportantDisclaimer



This is not an exhaustive list! There are errors you can make that I am not yet aware of. This is just a list of errors I have seen and instructions on how to avoid them. When in doubt, ask Michael or one of the TAs!
